name: Art Pepper
abstract: Arthur Edward Pepper Jr. (September 1, 1925 – June 15, 1982) was an American jazz musician, most known as an alto saxophonist.

concerts.label: Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music
Stan Kenton: Innovations in Modern Music

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: I had already got to meet and, you know as a teenager gotten to meet and play with Art Pepper... I had befriended him [Lester Koenig] because of my friendship with — or he befriended me because of my friendship with Art Pepper.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Porrello
about_quotes.evidence: in that band that I played with that was Maynard and Buddy Childers and Conte Candoli — all the heavyweights you know — Bob Cooper, Art Pepper, all those guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Porrello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, March 6, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: I had him in a small band with Art Pepper and Cat Anderson... We worked in San Francisco around up there, and it was a real good little band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Pell
about_quotes.evidence: This ensemble found a commercial niche in the jazz market and featured sidemen included Benny Carter, Pepper Adams and Art Pepper.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Pell interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 11, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: I used him on the Hoagy Carmichael album. I used to play with him in the Spanish bands
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: it was during the time I had Art Pepper... playing saxaphones at the Club Alabam
